vocab.meaning: sự tự chủ khi cầm lái
vocab.example: I prefer being the driver because I relish the sense of autonomy behind the wheel, deciding exactly which route to take.

vocab.meaning: có tính trị liệu, giúp thư giãn đầu óc
vocab.example: For me, driving on an open highway is quite therapeutic; it helps me clear my mind after a long day at work.
I’d much rather be a passenger so I can simply recline the seat and catch up on some much-needed rest.

vocab.meaning: giao phó trách nhiệm (cho người khác)
vocab.example: In heavy traffic, I prefer to delegate the responsibility of navigating to someone else while I relax in the back.

vocab.meaning: tầm nhìn bao quát (thường từ vị trí lái)
vocab.example: Being in the driver's seat gives me a commanding view of the road, which makes me feel much more secure.

vocab.meaning: người ngồi sau hay chỉ đạo lái xe (thường gây khó chịu)
vocab.example: I try my best not to be a backseat driver when my friends are at the helm, as I know how annoying unsolicited advice can be.

vocab.meaning: cầm lái, nắm quyền điều khiển
vocab.example: I feel far more at ease when I am at the helm, as I trust my own reflexes more than anyone else's.

vocab.meaning: niềm vui gián tiếp (thưởng thức thông qua người khác)
vocab.example: I get a bit of vicarious enjoyment from watching the scenery go by while someone else handles the stress of the road.
Navigating through a bustling city center can be nerve-wracking, so I’m happy to let a professional driver take over.
While being a passenger is quite sedentary, it provides the perfect opportunity to dive into a good book or listen to a podcast.
